title: Rubacer velutinum
intro: Rubus occiniflorus, also known as Rubus velutinum, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, supporting cardiovascular health and potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers. The bioactive constituents of Rubus velutinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rubus velutinum is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
